_meta.purpose: Immutable, append-only ledger of pre-committed standing nowcasts for outbreak headline counts. Sibling to data/calibration-ledger.json. Each nowcast pre-commits a best-estimate-today for a named headline quantity on a specific data-as-of target date, with a point and predictive intervals, before the official figure for that date arrives. Resolution is event-driven: the nowcast is auto-scored when editions carrying the target date's data_as_of appear, per the latency-observatory's upper-tail timing. Brier and weighted interval scores accumulate as appended ledger entries; a rolling track record becomes visible within about a week of cadence. Spec: labs/lovs-public-goods/latency-nowcast-spec.md v0.4.
doctrine: Pre-commit. A nowcast's point, intervals, target_date, and resolution trigger are never edited after commit.
Carry forward. A daily refresh re-emits the pre-committed unresolved nowcasts verbatim; never re-derive the point or intervals from the model run that comes after pin time.
Append only. New nowcasts are added as new entries with explicit issued_at timestamps. Resolutions are appended as new entries; the original pre-committed point and intervals are never altered.

Resolve event-driven. A nowcast resolves when editions carrying its target_date as their data_as_of arrive, reconciled to a set (min, max, primary). Score with WIS (Bracher et al. 2021) for intervals and Brier (Brier 1950) for any directional/threshold call. Resolution deadline is the upper tail (e.g. p90) of the latency observatory's total_visibility_lag distribution for the source set used.
Co-present with authoritative. Every public render of a nowcast must show the authoritative count alongside it. The nowcast complements; it never replaces.
Open-data only. Inputs are aggregate already-published counts plus the manifest's already-published timestamps. No restricted covariates.
_meta.id_derivation: nowcast_id = 'nc:lovs:' + quantity + ':' + target_date. The quantity slug must be one of the registered series in registered_quantities below. The target_date is a bare YYYY-MM-DD.
_meta.consumed_by: Future refresh_nowcast.py or refresh_pipeline.py extension carries pre-committed unresolved entries forward into the snapshot's nowcast block. See spec section 7.
_meta.licensing: CC BY 4.0 (per LICENSES.md) covers this file's schema, structure, and authored annotations. Nowcast values are original derived outputs of the latency-observatory + reporting-delay model.
_meta.phase_1_scope: This scaffold creates the ledger schema and registers the confirmed-cumulative headline quantity per spec section 8 (the confirmed-and-suspected composite is retired with the suspected tier). The dark-date detector, Bayesian right-truncation model (Hohle and an der Heiden 2014; McGough et al. 2020; Gunther et al. 2021), WIS scorer (Bracher et al. 2021), Brier scorer (Brier 1950), backtester, and website surface land in subsequent phases per the engineering-plan decomposition in .process/2026-05-26-pre-refresh-decisions/follow-up-after-pushback.md.
